description: Build macro readiness from compiler2's existing function-definition/lowered-body/dispatch/backend machinery without sending defmacro through the runtime-root closure/materialization ladder. The output of this ticket is the compile-time backend artifact the interpreter actually needs; it is not a runtime executable pipeline product. Signal: BuildMacroExecutable (or its replacement) no longer schedules SeedRoot/AnalyzeActivation/SealSemanticClosure/MaterializeRoot/DeriveAbiReady/DeriveEmissionReady for macros, and macro-ready tests prove the interpreter still runs quote/unquote over the source heap.
title: Compiler2 macro backend: produce interpreter-ready backend directly from defined macro bodies
